[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2015-03-17
[TITLE]: Is This a Picture of a Bunny at a L'Oreal Animal Testing Lab? | Snopes.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: In early 2013 an image of a suffering rabbit began to circulate online accompanied by a claim attributing the animal's visibly poor health to laboratory testing conducted by the L'Oreal cosmetics and beauty company. A version of the picture was...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: In early 2013 an image of a suffering rabbit began to circulate online accompanied by a claim attributing the animal's visibly poor health to laboratory testing conducted by the L'Oreal cosmetics and beauty company. 
 A version of the picture was posted to Twitter in February 2013, and in April 2013 the rabbit photo and rumor circulated on Tumblr and was also posted to Reddit: 
 This bunny at a L'Oreal Lab. I will not buy any #L'Oreal products anymore! pic.twitter.com/XV2LE0AG 
 — jose (@jjose1202) February 9, 2013 
 When the photograph and claim spread across several social networks including Facebook in March 2015, L'Oreal responded on their own Facebook page, explaining that their cosmetics were never tested on animals except for instances in which such tests are mandated by law:
